How to use
About
Drafthaus
Built by Jason Craig: songwriter, producer, perpetual work-in-progress.
Singer / Guitarist Producer Music Production, Toronto Film School BA Psychology VP, CTS & Associates Toronto Area Director, Rockstar Music Central

I've been writing, performing, and recording music since I was 13, tinkering away in my parents' house with whatever I could get my hands on. Over the years I played in bands, landed as a singer and guitarist in Iduna, and still show up every Thursday night to write with those guys, make noise, and catch up. Some things don't change.

Along the way I picked up a BA in psychology, went to Toronto Film School for music production, and made my way through pretty much every recording setup you've heard of: a Tascam 4-track cassette when I was just getting started, Audacity when I got my first computer, then Nuendo, Cubase, Pro Tools, and finally Logic, where I've stayed. By day I work as VP of Client Services and Strategic Partnerships at CTS & Associates, and as Toronto Area Director at Rockstar Music Central, focused on growth, partnerships, and teaching music to the next generation of players.

Music has been a constant through all of it. But here's the honest problem: I have a million unfinished Logic projects. Ideas, fragments, sketches that never went anywhere. Not because they weren't good, but because the moment I opened a DAW I was already thinking about production instead of the song.

That's why I built Drafthaus.

I needed a place, anywhere, any device, to just write. Put the idea down, work out the chords, lyrics, and structure without the pressure of a full session staring back at me. And when something was actually ready, export it: MIDI, audio notes, chord charts, and bring it into Logic with something real to work from.

Drafthaus is that place. It's a songwriter's workbench, not a DAW. The draft comes first. Everything else comes after. I hope it's as useful to you as it's been to me keeping all my ideas organized.

Made with too many late nights & not enough finished songs. Jason
Hit Enter to Expand
Hit Enter to Expand
🎵
Welcome to Drafthaus
Your songwriter's workspace — lyrics, chords, structure, and recording all in one place.

This quick guide walks you through the key features so you can hit the ground running.
📋
Your Draft List
The Draft List is your home base. Every song starts here.

• Hit + New Draft to create a song
• Drag songs between projects to stay organized
• Use ⋯ on any row to share, rename, or delete
• Press N for a quick new draft, / to search
✏️
Lyrics & Chord Annotations
Open a song and switch to Lyrics view. Add sections like Verse and Chorus, then click ✏ Edit lyrics to write.

Drag chords from the right-hand palette directly onto syllables — the chord floats above that word in your chart.

Hit Rehearse for a clean, distraction-free performance view.
🎹
Your Production Tools
The bottom bar gives you four tools that sync to your song's BPM:

4-Track — Record and overdub audio takes per section
Keyboard — Play and record melodies with your MIDI device, your computer keyboard (keys 1–8 once a key is assigned to a song), or the on-screen keys
Sequencer — Program drum and chord patterns. Load built-in drum presets to start fast, then customize per section
Looper — Layer live loops on top of your song

Open the Mix panel to balance levels across all four.
⬡
Sharing & Shortcuts
Share any draft via ⋯ → Share on the song row — collaborators can view or edit in real time.

A few shortcuts worth knowing:
HDraft list LLyrics view RRehearse mode BBackend T / K / S / O4-Track / Keyboard / Sequencer / Looper TabTap tempo (tap to the beat) ↓ ArrowGo to draft list ↵ EnterExpand active pane / revert EscClose / go back NNew draft /Search
3
Bluetooth Sync
Trim latency on save
Offset ms
Removes the first N ms from each recording to compensate for Bluetooth input latency.
Place your Bluetooth mic near your speaker. Keep the room quiet. The app will play 5 clicks and measure how long they take to reach the mic.
